title: Template updates: TASK_PACKET / REFINEMENT / REFINEMENT_CONTRACT carry HBR fields by default
goal: Update the three canonical authoring templates so new packets auto-emit HBR fields. TASK_PACKET template adds `hbr.tags_declared` + `hbr.not_applicable_overrides` + `acceptance_matrix.hbr` skeleton. REFINEMENT template adds an `hbr_pillar_review` section. REFINEMENT_CONTRACT template adds `hbr_obligations` array per MT scope.
expected_diff_shape: Edit existing template files under .GOV/templates/ (or equivalent location — confirm via Glob); preserve all existing template fields; add the new HBR-related fields with sensible defaults.
unblock_conditions: MT-002 hydrator + MT-038 role-protocol linkage merged so the template defaults reflect the live wiring.
implementation_notes: Confirm template paths via Glob before editing (.GOV/templates/ is the most likely location; if not, check .GOV/roles_shared/templates/). TASK_PACKET_TEMPLATE.json: add `hbr: { tags_declared: ["<auto-infer-from-touched-paths>"], not_applicable_overrides: [] }` and `acceptance_matrix: { schema_version: 1, hbr: [], hbr_not_applicable: [] }` to the default packet shape. REFINEMENT_TEMPLATE.json: add a new section `hbr_pillar_review: { INT: { applicable: bool, evidence_path }, SWARM: {...}, VIS: {...}, QUIET: {...}, MAN: {...} }` so refinement authors review each pillar before signature. REFINEMENT_CONTRACT_TEMPLATE.json: each MT entry gets `hbr_obligations: ["<HBR-IDs the MT must satisfy>"]` as a required field. All edits are additive — preserve every existing field with its current default. If templates do not yet exist as named files, document the discovery in implementation notes and skip rather than invent; the operator-driven RGF that defines templates owns their creation. Tests: Node script `.GOV/roles_shared/checks/template-hbr-fields.mjs` validates each template file has the new HBR fields; gov-check sub-check.
risk_if_missed: Without template defaults, new WPs ship without HBR fields and require post-hoc retrofit; A.1 enforcement falls short of authoring-time coverage.
validator_focus: Templates are extended additively (no removed fields); HBR fields have sensible defaults that survive a no-op packet creation; if templates do not exist as files, the MT documents the discovery rather than fabricating templates.

minimum_controls: Edits are additive; no existing field removed.
If templates do not exist, MT documents the discovery and exits without fabricating.
HBR field defaults are minimal (empty arrays, not pre-populated) to avoid prejudicing applicability.
